zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gale於1618年11月11日出生在英國英格蘭倫敦阿爾蓋特。她和Thomas Root Sr.至少有1個兒子。她去世於1691年11月25日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾諾薩普頓享年73歲。

nameMeanings.name: Gale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French gal, gale, galle ‘cock, cockerel’.

English: habitational name from a minor place called with either Middle English gale, gagel (Old English gagel) ‘bog myrtle’, or with Middle English gale ‘fishery paying dues to the lord’ (Old English gafol ‘tax’?) such as Gale in Bickington (Devon) and The Gale, the name of a fishery in Ham and Stone (Gloucestershire).

English: topographic name from Middle English gale, gail (Old Norse geil) ‘ravine, narrow lane’, or a habitational name from a place so named such as Gale in Littleborough, Rochdale (Lancashire), or possibly High Gale in Tatham or Tunstall (Lancashire). With the sense ‘narrow lane’ it is found in several street names in medieval York.

variants.name: Gaylor
meanings: English: occupational name for a jailer, from Middle English gaioler, gaoler, gailer (Old French gayolierre, gaiolere, jaioleur). See also Gale 4.
Americanized form of French   Gaillard  . Compare   Gaylord   and   Gayler  .
History: The surname Gaylor of French origin (see 2 above) is associated in particular with the Huguenots (see Gaylord ).

variants.name: Galer
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the village of Gali (Belarus, standard transliteration Hali), Galy (Ukraine, standard transliteration Haly), or Gało (northeastern Poland), formed with Yiddish gentilic suffix -er.

variants.name: Gail
meanings: German: variant of   Geil  . It is also found in France (Lorraine).

variants.name: Gayles
meanings: English: variant of Gale , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Possibly also an Americanized form of German Gölz (see Goelz ).

variants.name: Gayle
meanings: English: variant of   Gale  . This form of the surname is common in Jamaica.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gale was born on 11 November 1618, in Aldgate, London, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Thomas Root Sr.. She died on 25 November 1691, in Northampton, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 73.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gale(은)는 1618년 11월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 알드가트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Root Sr.(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1691년 11월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 노르섬프턴에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gale родилась 11 ноября 1618 г. в местности алджэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Thomas Root Sr. Она умерла 25 ноября 1691 г. в местности нортамптон, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 73 лет.
